title: Living in Boulder Vlog 5 - 19 -2017: More Church Fathers

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a brief life update from Boulder, noting he is deep into reading Church Fathers as part of a project to read all books from the ancient Western world. He discusses how prolific the Church Fathers are and how long the project is taking, and shares excitement about a voice-to-text/text-to-speech app called Voice Reading that he wants to use with Wikipedia articles and potentially for transcribing his videos.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Vastly underestimated how prolific they are; they may have more text than everything else in the ancient world combined
Optimistic he can finish in another week once he powers through the Church Fathers
Very excited about it as a productivity tool for consuming long texts and Wikipedia articles

claims.claim: I've been too busy to vlog for a few days.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm trying to read all the books from the ancient Western world.

claims.claim: I'm wondering if the church fathers wrote more text than everything else in the ancient world combined.

claims.claim: I originally planned to finish reading by last Sunday, but it's already the next Saturday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I underestimated how many books there are in history.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm still super fired up and plan to go another week to finish.

claims.subject: church fathers
claims.claim: Once I power through the church fathers, the rest will be easier.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Having transcripts would allow me to link to parts of history I talk about.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been listening to Wikipedia articles about Rome using a voice reading program.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got a program called Voice Reading that reads Wikipedia articles aloud.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The voice reading program reads very fast, which I like.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The program can read really big pages, like entire books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to try reading an entire book with the voice reading program soon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The program stopped when I tried to read a 3600-page text, maybe because I need the Pro version.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm super interested in finding out how many pages of text exist from the ancient world.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to categorize the pages of ancient texts by subject and link them to their original sources.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think linking all the texts to their original pages would be really cool.

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, V-LOG, May 20th. It took a few days since the last one because I've been just too busy, I guess. I'm trying to do all of the books from the ancient, ancient Western world, and it's, um, those, uh, church fathers are, whoo!
Um, those church fathers are very, very, very, uh, prolific. I'm still hacking through them. Right now I'm doing, um, The Pastor of Hermas by Clement of Rome, I think.

So, uh, yeah, I didn't realize there were so many. Uh, I, I'm curious, I'll find out pretty soon, but, um, I'm wondering what has more text, um, in the ancient world. Um, the church fathers or everything else but combined.
I mean, they saved a lot of it. Um, so, uh, yeah, I mean, I originally was just going to go until last Sunday, and now it's already Saturday, the next Saturday.
So, um, yeah, I mean, I'll do tomorrow, and I still won't have the end in sight. Um, maybe I underestimated how many books there are in history.
So, but, I mean, I'm super fired up still, so I think I'll, I'll just go another week, um, after this. You know, I think in another week I could definitely finish it,
Because once I power through the church fathers, it'll be all downhill after that. Um, yeah, I'm going to put it on the website.
I want to, uh, try the, uh, the voice, voice to text. I'm getting super fired up about that.
I had to download this new thing for it on my computer. So, but it looks like it'll, it may be here, so, enhanced dictation, so.
I want to see if I can get it, see how I can get that to work, if I can get it to write on a paper, because then I might get started with, um, putting transcripts of my videos on my website.
And because that way, like, I could provide a link to it, and then whatever I talk about, I could have a link to whatever, like, you know, part of history I talk to.
At least once I get the books up on my website, they're not on my website yet.
But, yeah, that would be interesting, if you can make it work so it's not time-consuming.
So, um, I've been, um, listening to, uh, uh, yeah, Wikipedia articles about Rome.
Stuff related, whew, stuff related to Rome.
I got, um, the, I don't know, I don't know how long this has been around, but I should have tried to get it earlier, because this is amazing.
I got this, uh, voice, text-to-voice program called, um, voice reading, and then I got the, the, um, the Wikipedia app.
And so, when you go to a Wikipedia, um, article, you just hit the button for voice reading, and it reads it to you.
Um, so that's amazing.
So, um, I want to find out how fast this thing reads, and see how, what it is compared to, uh, the, uh, the Mac.
And, uh, but it seems about the same.
It's pretty fast, which is what I like.
And, um, yeah, you can try to go through, uh, entire, uh, it could read, like, really big pages.
Like, you could have it read entire books, and so, like, all day long.
Finish the book all day with this thing.
I want to try that pretty soon.
But it freezes.
I tried to put Livy on here, and it, it read a little bit, and then it stopped and froze.
But Livy's, like, 3,600 pages.
So, um, and I didn't see a part where I could pause it, but maybe that's in the pro version.
Maybe I'll get the pro version.
I'll have to, uh, look and see what that has.
But, uh, anyway, so, um, uh, yeah, I'm super, super, ooh, interested to see how much, how many pages of text there is in the ancient world.
And then how many pages in the different categories.
And, uh, the names of all of, and, you know, the subjects and everything.
And, and what it's all about, and where all the, you know, the stuff is.
And then maybe even do some pages linking.
Just because I think that's really cool.
Like, all the laws have them linked to where they're actually written on the original, uh, page.
But, anyway, I think I've talked long enough.
Good night.
